series_description: This series contains license renewal applications, which contains licensees name, SS#, license number, address of licensee, and signature of licensee.
Continuing Education applications, attendance lists, material relative to specific compliance period.
These files provide proof of atttempt of licensure/incomplete files for embalmer and/or funeral director applicants. May contain college transcripts.
retention_statement: Paper: Incomplete records - retain for 10 years

series_description: A) This license provides proof of apprenticeship, examination, and licensure in Ohio as a funeral director. B) This license is reference in cases of reciprocity, and other board action.

series_title: FUNERAL DIRECTOR'S LICENSE APPLICATION HISTORY FILE
series_description: This file contains: 1) Funeral Director's application for registration [Form F-1]; 2) Funeral Director's certificate of registration [Form F-2]; 3) Funeral Director's certificate of beginning of apprenticeship [Form F-3]; 4) Funeral Director's apprenticeship card [Form F-4]; 5) Funeral Director's termination of apprenticeship [Form F-5]; 6) Certification of Funeral Directing [Form f-6]; 7) Application for funeral directors license [Form F-7]; 8) Examination Grade; 9) Copies of official correspondence from the Board of Embalmers and Funeral Directors notifying licensee of his failure to renew license and other formal actions of the Board; 10) Registered mail receipts on official correspondence sent to licensee. The above state files are maintained to insure that all licensed funeral director's have adequately qualified for a Funeral Director's License.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until death of licensee or revocation of license by Board of Embalmers and Funeral Directors.

series_description: INSPECTION REPORTS FOR FUNERAL HOMES, CREMATORIES, AND EMBALMING FACILITIES.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the most recent 10 years and all non-compliance inspection reports in appropriate file folders until close of facility or new license

series_description: A) These licenses provide proof of licensure of inactive embalmers and funeral direcdtors. B) This file serves as refence in action taken by the Board; e.g. - re-istatement.
retention_statement: Paper: Lapsed - Retain until notified of death or after 30 years. To be reviewed by State prior to disposal.

series_description: File maintained on all applicants applying for license as Embalmer or Funeral Director. All applicants must successfully complete apprenticeship program prior ro the issuance of Embalmers or Funeral Directors licnese. This file contains: 1) Application for Registration; 2) Certification of Apprenticeship; 3) Certificate of Registration; 4) Apprenticeship Pocket Card; 5) Certification of embalmer and funeral director; 6) Termination of apprenticeship (completed by master embalmer and apprentice); 7) Apprentice record card; 8) Master Embalmers or Funeral Directors quarterly progress report; 9) Apprentice quarterly progress case report; 10) Apprentice report of personnel interview with member of the Board of Embalmers and Funeral Directors.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area until completion of apprenticeship program. Upon completion of apprenticeship program. Upon completion of apprenticeship program, incorporate Items 1 - 7 above in Embalmers and Funeral Directors License application History File. Items 8-10, Summarize and record on forms E-8 or Form F-6 as appropriate

series_description: A seller of preneed funeral contracts who is subject to sections 4717.31 to 4717.38 of the Revised Code shall file an annual report relating to all existing funded preneed contracts as of December thirty-first of the prior calendar year. The report shall be submitted on a form provided by the board and shall include, but not be limited to the following: name of beneficiary, account or policy number, account balance, and activity on each trust account or insurance policy. All personal identifying information shall be redacted should a public records request actualize.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years or until audited by agency and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value to agency

series_description: This report lists the number of test conducted each year and the grade results of those examined as reported by private testing agency. Report contains the date test given, type of test conducted, name of individual taking test, and test score. Section 4743.02 of the Ohio Revised Code requires that all examinations be available for public inspection for a period of ninety days after announcement of grades. Since the actual examinations are disposed of after this ninety day period, this report serves as an official record of examinations conducted and the result.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy when determined of no further value.

series_description: This file contains: 1) Embalmers application for registration. [Form E-1]; 2) Embalmers certificate of registration [Form E-2]; 3) Application for embalming license [Form E-3]; 4) Embalmers certificate of apprenticeship [Form E-4]; 5) Embalmers certificate of beginning of apprenticeship [Form E-5]; 6) Embalmers termination of apprenticeship [Form E-6]; 7) Certification of embalming [Form E-7]; 8) Examination grade; 9) Copies of official correspondence from the Board of Embalmers and Funeral Directors notifying license of his failure to renew license and other formal action of the Board; 10) Registered mail receipts on official correspondence sent to licensees. The above stated files are maintained to insure that all licensed embalmers have adequately qualified for an Embalmer's License.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until death of licensee or revocation of license by Board of Embalmers and Funeral Directors.

series_description: A) This license provides proof of apprenticeship, examination, and licensure in Ohio as a embalmer. B) This license is reference in cases of reciprocity, and other board action.
